questions.question: Explain Flat Shading and list its advantages and limitations.
keyPoints: Flat shading computes one normal per polygon and applies lighting once for the entire face
Advantages: Fast computation, simple to implement
Limitations: Produces faceted appearance, cannot show smooth curves or specular highlights accurately

questions.question: Describe the steps involved in Gouraud Shading algorithm.
keyPoints: Step 1: Calculate surface normals at each vertex
Step 2: Compute vertex intensities using illumination model
Step 3: Interpolate intensities across polygon surface using bilinear interpolation
Step 4: Display resulting shaded polygon

questions.question: What is the difference between Gouraud Shading and Phong Shading?
keyPoints: Gouraud interpolates colors/intensities; Phong interpolates normals
Gouraud computes lighting at vertices; Phong computes at each pixel
Phong produces better specular highlights; Gouraud may miss them
Phong is computationally more expensive than Gouraud

questions.question: Explain Lambert's Cosine Law in the context of diffuse reflection.
keyPoints: States that intensity of light reaching a surface is proportional to cos(θ)
Where θ is angle between light direction and surface normal
Formula: I = I_light × k_d × cos(θ) = I_light × k_d × (N·L)
When surface faces away from light (θ > 90°), intensity is zero

questions.question: Write the Phong Illumination Model equation and explain each term.
keyPoints: I = I_a × k_a + I_d × k_d × (N·L) + I_s × k_s × (R·V)^n
I_a × k_a: Ambient component (base illumination)
I_d × k_d × (N·L): Diffuse component (Lambertian reflection)
I_s × k_s × (R·V)^n: Specular component (phong highlight)
n is shininess exponent controlling highlight size

questions.question: How is the surface normal vector calculated for a polygon?
keyPoints: Take two edge vectors of the polygon: E1 = V2 - V1, E2 = V3 - V1
Compute cross product: N_raw = E1 × E2
Normalize: N = N_raw / |N_raw|
This gives unit vector perpendicular to polygon surface

questions.question: Explain why Phong Shading produces better results than Gouraud Shading for specular highlights.
keyPoints: Gouraud shading interpolates computed vertex intensities
If specular highlight occurs in middle of polygon (not at vertices), it is missed
Phong shading interpolates normals and computes lighting per pixel
Every pixel gets accurate lighting calculation including specular component
Result: sharper, more realistic specular highlights

questions.question: Explain in detail the three main shading methods: Flat, Gouraud, and Phong. Compare them in terms of computational cost, visual quality, and applications.
keyPoints: Flat Shading: One color per polygon, uses face normal, fastest but faceted look
Gouraud Shading: Interpolates vertex intensities, captures smooth color gradients, misses mid-polygon highlights
Phong Shading: Interpolates normals, per-pixel lighting, best visual quality
Computational cost: Flat < Gouraud < Phong
Applications: Flat for low-end systems and stylized rendering; Gouraud for real-time applications; Phong for photorealistic rendering

questions.question: Derive and explain the Phong Illumination Model. How does each component (ambient, diffuse, specular) contribute to the final surface color?
keyPoints: Phong model combines three lighting components
Ambient: I_a × k_a - constant term simulating scattered light, prevents pure black shadows
Diffuse: I_d × k_d × (N·L) - Lambertian reflection, depends on angle to light, matte surfaces
Specular: I_s × k_s × (R·V)^n - mirror-like reflection, creates highlights, shiny surfaces
Total intensity is sum of all components
Parameters: k_a, k_d, k_s are reflectance coefficients; n is shininess exponent

questions.question: Write an algorithm for implementing Gouraud Shading. Also explain how you would modify it to implement Phong Shading.
keyPoints: Gouraud Algorithm: 1) Input polygon vertices 2) Compute face normals 3) Calculate vertex normals (by averaging adjacent face normals) 4) Compute lighting intensity at each vertex using Phong model 5) For each scan line, interpolate intensity across edges then along scan line 6) Display pixels
Phong Modification: Instead of interpolating intensity, interpolate vertex normals across polygon, compute lighting at each pixel using interpolated normal, allows per-pixel specular highlights
Key difference: Gouraud is vertex shader, Phong is fragment shader

questions.question: Explain the concept of surface normals in polygonal shading. How are they computed? How does smooth shading differ from flat shading in terms of normals?
keyPoints: Surface normal is unit vector perpendicular to polygon face
Computation: Cross product of two edge vectors: N = (V2-V1) × (V3-V1), normalized
Flat shading: Uses face normal for entire polygon - gives faceted appearance
Smooth shading: Uses vertex normals (averaged from adjacent faces) - gives smooth curved appearance
To achieve smooth shading, vertices must share normals across adjacent polygons
Without shared normals, mesh appears faceted despite shading algorithm
